Notes from Neurotica

  • Home
  • Archive

Sleep (Sonnet)


Sleep is a deterrent, It’s denial and defeat; An escape from what is current, Masking regret in self-deceit.

It begins in bouts of self-hate, searching for a succinct answer; optimism begins to abate, the mind becomes reliant on the contemptuous cancer.

The mind unravels into open warfare, Battles on the validity of being human are waged; Fear farms the mental terrain, plowing inaction square, Fences installed, questions of self doubt are left to graze.

The rich rivers of optimism of ones youth begin to run dry; Glassy eyes become estuaries for tears of lost time, Yet the clock only continues, hours begin to crucify. Destructive thoughts gather to be primed.

Mediocrity melts into decay. Consciousness falters recursively. Dreamless regret looms headway. Emotions embrace with discourtesy.

No matter what the future case is, Sleep embraces.

īn nīz bogzarad
